description: African-American lynching victim (1941–1955)
description_wiki: Emmett Louis Till (July 25, 1941 – August 28, 1955) was an African-American boy who, at 14 years old, was abducted and lynched in Mississippi in 1955 after being accused of offending a white woman, Carolyn Bryant, in her family's grocery store.

description: protest campaign against racial segregation on buses in Montgomery, Alabama
description_wiki: The Montgomery bus boycott was a political and social protest campaign against the policy of racial segregation on the public transit system of Montgomery, Alabama.

description: public university in the United States

description: American activist
description_wiki: Autherine Juanita Lucy (October 5, 1929 – March 2, 2022) was an American activist who was the first African-American student to attend the University of Alabama, in 1956.

description: Civil rights activist (1922-2011)
description_wiki: Freddie Lee Shuttlesworth (born Freddie Lee Robinson, March 18, 1922 – October 5, 2011) was an American Baptist minister and civil rights activist who led fights against segregation and other forms of racism, during the civil rights movement.

description_wiki: Reverend Claude Herbert Oliver (February 28, 1925 – November 30, 2021) was an American pastor and civil rights activist who was the secretary of the Birmingham Inter-Citizens Committee from 1960 to 1965.

description: American 1960s civil rights campaign
description_wiki: The sit-in movement, sit-in campaign, or student sit-in movement, was a wave of sit-ins that followed the Greensboro sit-ins on February 1, 1960, led by students – Ezell Blair Jr., David Richmond, Franklin McCain, and Joseph McNeil – at North Carolina Agricultural and Technical Institute (A&T).

description: U.S. activists who rode interstate buses in interracial groups to show the continuing prevalence of segregation
description_wiki: Freedom Riders were civil rights activists who rode interstate buses into the segregated Southern United States in 1961 and subsequent years to challenge the non-enforcement of the United States Supreme Court decisions Morgan v.

description: largest student-led civil rights organization during the American Civil Rights Movement
description_wiki: The Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee, and later the Student National Coordinating Committee (SNCC, pronounced  SNIK), was the principal channel of student commitment in the United States to the civil rights movement during the 1960s.

description: United States civil rights organization

description: city in Ohio, United States; county seat of Hamilton County
